lines.english: There were no demi-gods or temples, no cows or Gaayatri prayer.
There were no castes or social classes, no religious robes, no Brahmin or Kh'shaatriya.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਕੋ ਮੁਲਾ ਨਾ ਕੋ ਕਾਜੀ ॥
lines.translit: naa ko mulaa naa ko kaajee |
lines.english: There was no Mullah, there was no Qazi.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੋਮ ਜਗ ਨਹੀ ਤੀਰਥਿ ਨਾਵਣੁ ਨਾ ਕੋ ਪੂਜਾ ਲਾਇਦਾ ॥੧੦॥
lines.translit: hom jag nahee teerath naavan naa ko poojaa laaeidaa |10|
lines.english: There were no burnt offerings, no ceremonial feasts, no cleansing rituals at sacred shrines of pilgrimage; no one worshipped in adoration. ||10||
There was no Shaykh, or pilgrims to Mecca.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਈਅਤਿ ਰਾਉ ਨ ਹਉਮੈ ਦੁਨੀਆ ਨਾ ਕੋ ਕਹਣੁ ਕਹਾਇਦਾ ॥੧੧॥
lines.translit: reeat raau na haumai duneea naa ko kehan kahaaeidaa |11|
lines.english: There was no king or subjects, and no worldly egotism; no one spoke of himself. ||11||
There were no friends or companions, no semen or blood.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਸਾਹੁ ਆਪੇ ਵਣਜਾਰਾ ਸਾਚੇ ਏਹੋ ਭਾਇਦਾ ॥੧੨॥
lines.translit: aape saahu aape vanajaaraa saache eho bhaaeidaa |12|
lines.english: He Himself is the banker, and He Himself is the merchant. Such is the Pleasure of the Will of the True Lord. ||12||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਾਉ ਨ ਭਗਤੀ ਨਾ ਸਿਵ ਸਕਤੀ ॥
lines.translit: bhaau na bhagatee naa siv sakatee |
lines.english: There was no love or devotion, no Shiva or Shakti - no energy or matter.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਤਾ ਬਕਤਾ ਆਪਿ ਅਗੋਚਰੁ ਆਪੇ ਅਲਖੁ ਲਖਾਇਦਾ ॥੧੩॥
lines.translit: kahataa bakataa aap agochar aape alakh lakhaaeidaa |13|
lines.english: The Unfathomable Lord Himself was the speaker and the preacher; the unseen Lord Himself saw everything. ||13||
There was no recitation of the Puraanas, no sunrise or sunset.
There were no Vedas, Korans or Bibles, no Simritees or Shaastras.
Without any supporting power, He sustained the universe.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਤਿਸੁ ਭਾਣਾ ਤਾ ਜਗਤੁ ਉਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: jaa tis bhaanaa taa jagat upaaeaa |
lines.english: When He so willed, He created the world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾ ਬਿਸਨੁ ਮਹੇਸੁ ਉਪਾਏ ਮਾਇਆ ਮੋਹੁ ਵਧਾਇਦਾ ॥੧੪॥
lines.translit: brahamaa bisan mahes upaae maaeaa mohu vadhaaeidaa |14|
lines.english: He created Brahma, Vishnu and Shiva; He fostered enticement and attachment to Maya. ||14||
How rare is that person who listens to the Word of the Guru's Shabad.
He created the creation, and watches over it; the Hukam of His Command is over all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖੰਡ ਬ੍ਰਹਮੰਡ ਪਾਤਾਲ ਅਰੰਭੇ ਗੁਪਤਹੁ ਪਰਗਟੀ ਆਇਦਾ ॥੧੫॥
lines.translit: khandd brahamandd paataal aranbhe gupatahu paragattee aaeidaa |15|
lines.english: He formed the planets, solar systems and nether regions, and brought what was hidden to manifestation. ||15||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਾਚਿ ਰਤੇ ਬਿਸਮਾਦੀ ਬਿਸਮ ਭਏ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਇਦਾ ॥੧੬॥੩॥੧੫॥
lines.translit: naanak saach rate bisamaadee bisam bhe gun gaaeidaa |16|3|15|
lines.english: O Nanak, those who are attuned to the Truth are wonderstruck; singing His Glorious Praises, they are filled with wonder. ||16||3||15||
This understanding comes from the Perfect Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਾ ਕਾ ਅੰਤੁ ਨ ਜਾਣੈ ਕੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: taa kaa ant na jaanai koee |
lines.english: No one knows His limits.
The Merciful Lord has established His True Home.
Maaroo, First Mehl:
He Himself created the creation, remaining unattached.
The Creator established the nine gates.
In the Tenth Gate, is the dwelling of the infinite, unseen Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉਣ ਪਾਣੀ ਅਗਨੀ ਕਾ ਬੰਧਨੁ ਕਾਇਆ ਕੋਟੁ ਰਚਾਇਦਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: paun paanee aganee kaa bandhan kaaeaa kott rachaaeidaa |1|
lines.english: Binding together air, water and fire, He created the fortress of the body. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਇਰ ਸਪਤ ਭਰੇ ਜਲਿ ਨਿਰਮਲਿ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮੈਲੁ ਨ ਲਾਇਦਾ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: saaeir sapat bhare jal niramal guramukh mail na laaeidaa |2|
lines.english: The seven seas are overflowing with the Ambrosial Water; the Gurmukhs are not stained with filth. ||2||
The lamps of the sun and the moon fill all with light.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋਤਿ ਸਰੂਪ ਸਦਾ ਸੁਖਦਾਤਾ ਸਚੇ ਸੋਭਾ ਪਾਇਦਾ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: jot saroop sadaa sukhadaataa sache sobhaa paaeidaa |3|
lines.english: The Giver of peace is forever the embodiment of Light; from the True Lord, glory is obtained. ||3||
Within the fortress are the stores and markets; the business is transacted there.
Creating them, He beholds His own glorious greatness.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਰਤਨੁ ਵਿਸਾਹੇ ਲੇਵੈ ਆਪੇ ਕੀਮਤਿ ਪਾਇਦਾ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: aape ratan visaahe levai aape keemat paaeidaa |4|
lines.english: He Himself buys the jewel, and He Himself appraises its value. ||4||
The Supreme Merchant weighs with the perfect weights.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਰਬ ਕਲਾ ਲੇ ਆਪੇ ਰਹਿਆ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਕਿਸੈ ਬੁਝਾਇਦਾ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: sarab kalaa le aape rahiaa guramukh kisai bujhaaeidaa |5|
lines.english: He holds all powers, He is all-pervading; how few are those who, as Gurmukh, understand this. ||5||
The Appraiser appraises its value.
The Independent Lord is overflowing with His treasures.
The tyrannical Messenger of Death cannot strike him then.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਉ ਜਲ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਕਮਲੁ ਬਿਗਾਸੀ ਆਪੇ ਬਿਗਸਿ ਧਿਆਇਦਾ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: jiau jal antar kamal bigaasee aape bigas dhiaaeidaa |6|
lines.english: He blossoms forth like the lotus flower in the water; he blossoms forth in joyful meditation. ||6||
When He bestows His Glance of Grace, one meets the Perfect Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਮਿਲੈ ਤ ਪੂਰਾ ਪਾਈਐ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਪਦਾਰਥੁ ਪਾਇਦਾ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: satigur milai ta pooraa paaeeai prem padaarath paaeidaa |7|
lines.english: When they meet the True Guru, then they find the Perfect Lord; they obtain the treasure of Love. ||7||
He Himself rains down the Ambrosial Stream of jewels,
Diamonds, and rubies of priceless value.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਬ ਹੀ ਨ ਘਾਟਸਿ ਪੂਰਾ ਤੋਲੋ ॥
lines.translit: kab hee na ghaattas pooraa tolo |
lines.english: - his weight never decreases; he has perfect weight.
Whoever receives the priceless treasure of Love

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੇ ਕਾ ਵਾਪਾਰੀ ਹੋਵੈ ਸਚੋ ਸਉਦਾ ਪਾਇਦਾ ॥੮॥
lines.translit: sache kaa vaapaaree hovai sacho saudaa paaeidaa |8|
lines.english: The trader of Truth becomes true, and obtains the merchandise. ||8||
How rare are those who obtain the true merchandise.
Meeting the Perfect True Guru, one meets with the Lord.
